content:
Let's have a casual conversation about Education.

Please start by asking me this question in a natural, friendly way - as if you're genuinely interested in my answer:

"What field did you study in?"

After I respond, ask follow-up questions to go deeper into this topic (ask "why", "how long", "how often", etc.). Keep the conversation flowing naturally like a friendly chat, not an interview. Use a warm, encouraging tone.

If I seem hesitant or give short answers, gently encourage me to share more. If I go deep into a topic, explore it further with reflective questions (depth 3-4 level questions).

Remember: This is IELTS speaking practice, so help me express myself more fluently and with better vocabulary, but don't correct me directly - just model better language naturally in your responses.

That sounds like a great way to practice! Let's start.

So, what field did you study in?

Uh-huh. I mainly started in communication and journalism. So we any kind of topics that is really with communications, for example, marketing, affiliate marketing, which is the the industry that I'm currently listing. So I'm also really keen to explore yoga where we can combine communications, media, with sing yogas to become an influences of the yoga industry. Maybe that could be a really great ways to develop my my future career as well.

That's a really interesting combination — communication and journalism as a foundation, and then branching into marketing and affiliate marketing, which is where you're working now. And then yoga on top of that? That's such a unique blend!

So you're thinking of becoming a kind of influencer in the yoga space, using your communication skills to build a presence there. What is it about yoga that draws you to want to build a career around it?

So for yoga, I have always been fascinating, really with this sports. Always been really sporty ive. I first begin to get to know about yoga starting from my mom, and I've been starting to doing yoga and become a yogi since I was 18. And I would definitely want to explore more interest really with yoga and possibly to build front and interest to a career, which I think can be a really great foundations to build on because I have already have a lot of practice within yoga. So that could be a great tools that I can combine with my expertise with it, which is communications.

That's lovely — so yoga has been a part of your life for quite a while! Starting at 18 and sticking with it means you've built up years of practice, which is a really solid foundation to build on.

I love that your mom introduced you to it — was she a practitioner herself? And what was it about yoga that first clicked for you when you were 18? Was it more the physical side, or the mindfulness and breathing aspects?

Yes. My mom is a yoga teacher, so I have started she's basically a mentor of mine. And really with yoga, the first things that I want started with is definitely I want to lose some fat weights of of my body. But then I started to notice that it is not more than losing fat. It is also a mental treatments that you can really sense the connections between your mind and your body. Which is a really great ways to calm calm yourself, to soothe in yourself, which is the ways that I can feel the natures of it, and also the way that you can start to explore some really intense postures that also really intense me as well.

That's wonderful — having your mom as both a teacher and a mentor must make the practice feel really special and personal.

And I love how your journey evolved — starting with a physical goal like losing weight, and then discovering that deeper mind-body connection. That shift from "this is exercise" to "this is something that calms and soothes me" is such a powerful experience. The way you describe feeling the connection between your mind and body — that's really the heart of yoga, isn't it?

You mentioned intense postures too — are there any particular poses or styles of yoga that you've really fallen in love with over the years?
